title: Laelia × ingramii
taxonomy: Laelia × ingramii is a hybrid orchid, specifically a cross between two species of Laelia. It is a type of epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants or objects, typically in a tropical environment. The classification of Laelia × ingramii places it within the genus Laelia, which is part of the family Orchidaceae. Within the Laelia genus, Laelia × ingramii is considered to be a distinct species due to its unique characteristics and hybrid origins. Its classification is further supported by its resemblance to its parent species and its ability to reproduce.
uses: Laelia × ingramii is a hybrid orchid, and while there isn't much information available on its specific medicinal uses, certain orchid species have been studied for their potential health benefits.
